title: Protect a Route with BasicAuth Middleware
description:
## Protect a Route with BasicAuth Middleware

Traefik's `BasicAuth` middleware protects routes with HTTP Basic Authentication. Credentials are stored as htpasswd-formatted strings in a Kubernetes Secret, which the middleware references.

**Your task:**

In the `traefik-lab` namespace:
1. Create a Secret named `basic-auth-secret` with a key `users` containing the htpasswd entry: `admin:$apr1$xyz$abc123` (use this literal string — no real hashing needed for this exercise).
2. Create a `Middleware` named `basic-auth` that uses `BasicAuth` and references the secret `basic-auth-secret`.

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get middleware basic-auth -n traefik-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.basicAuth.secret}'
```

hints.title: Secret and Middleware YAML
hints.body: The BasicAuth middleware's spec.basicAuth.secret field must match the name of a Secret in the same namespace. The Secret must have a 'users' key with htpasswd-formatted content.
hints.command: kubectl create secret generic basic-auth-secret --from-literal=users='admin:$apr1$xyz$abc123' -n traefik-lab

hints.title: Creating the Middleware
hints.body: After creating the Secret, apply the Middleware referencing it.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: Middleware
metadata:
  name: basic-auth
  namespace: traefik-lab
spec:
  basicAuth:
    secret: basic-auth-secret
EOF

validation.description: Check that the BasicAuth middleware and its backing secret exist

commands.command: kubectl get secret basic-auth-secret -n traefik-lab -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get middleware basic-auth -n traefik-lab -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get middleware basic-auth -n traefik-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.basicAuth.secret}'
